task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace application window is open and visible on the screen with a blank canvas.

task_completion.description: Draw a rectangular sketch for the base
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D rectangle is drawn in the SolveSpace viewport.

task_completion.description: Constrain the rectangular base to 40mm by 30mm
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension constraints of '40' (or 40.00) and '30' (or 30.00) are visibly attached to the edges of the rectangle in the sketch.

task_completion.description: Create a 3D solid block from the sketch
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D rectangular sketch has been extruded into a 3D solid block (any depth is acceptable as no specific depth was requested).
A save dialog shows the file being saved as 'block.slvs' in the '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory, or the SolveSpace window title bar updates to show 'block.slvs'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the SolveSpace GUI rather than using terminal commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with SolveSpace menus, toolbars, or the drawing canvas.

integrity.description: The agent actually constructed the 3D model step-by-step.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a progression of screenshots showing the 2D sketch, the application of dimensions, and the 3D extrusion, rather than just opening a pre-made file.

integrity.description: The file was saved using the application's save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box from SolveSpace is visible in at least one screenshot.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a 3D solid block with a 40mm x 30mm base in SolveSpace and saving it to '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/block.slvs'. Note: The underlying evaluation script expects a 15mm extrusion depth, but this requirement was completely omitted from the user prompt. Therefore, you must accept ANY visible 3D extrusion depth as correct, provided the base is 40x30mm.
